title: Fresh Strawberry Mini-Cupcakes with White Chocolate Frosting
ingredients: 4 ounces white chocolate, chopped (do not use chips)
instructions: Insert cupcake liners into 40 mini-muffin cups.
Cream butter and sugar in medium bowl for 3-5 minutes or until fluffy.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Combine dry ingredients (flour, baking powder, salt) and add to creamed mixture, alternating with milk, and mix for 3 minutes.
Fill each mini-muffin cup with a generous 1 tablespoon of batter.
Bake for 16-18 minutes (or 20-24 minutes for regular-sized cupcakes).
Place white chocolate in top of double boiler set over barely simmering water and stir just until melted, smooth and just warm.
Beat cream cheese in large bowl until fluffy.
Beat in 3/4 cup powdered sugar, then warm chocolate.
In separate bowl, beat whipping cream until beginning to thicken.
Beat in 3/4 cup powdered sugar until medium-firm peaks form.
Fold whipped cream mixture into cream cheese mixture in 2 additions.
Cover and chill frosting in refrigerator for 1-2 hours or up to 4 days before using.
Frost cooled cupcakes and top each with a strawberry slice.
notes: Makes 12-16 regular-sized cupcakes or approximately 40 mini-cupcakes
White chocolate seizes easily - do not overheat when melting
White chocolate can be melted in microwave instead of double boiler, but watch very carefully and stir every 20 seconds
Do not use white chocolate chips - use chopped white chocolate instead
Frosting can be made 1-2 hours ahead or up to 4 days in advance
Originally made for a wedding reception
